plssem 0.1.1
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Categorical Models with plssem
    • Interaction Models with plssem
    • Linear Models with plssem
    • Multilevel Models with plssem
    • Ordinal Regression with plssem
    • Parallel Bootstrapping with plssem

Multilevel Models with plssem

Source: vignettes/multilevel-models.Rmd
multilevel-models.Rmd

This vignette shows examples of multilevel random slopes and intercept models, with both continuous and ordinal data.

Random Slopes Model 

slopes_model <- "
  X =~ x1 + x2 + x3
  Z =~ z1 + z2 + z3
  Y =~ y1 + y2 + y3
  W =~ w1 + w2 + w3
  Y ~ X + Z + (1 + X + Z | cluster)
  W ~ X + Z + (1 + X + Z | cluster)
"

Continuous Indicators 

fit_slopes_cont <- pls(
  slopes_model,
  data      = randomSlopes,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50
)
summary(fit_slopes_cont)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 3 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                   PLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          LINEAR
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                          5000
#>   Number of iterations                               3
#>   Number of latent variables                         4
#>   Number of observed variables                      18
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                   127.210
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                50
#>   SRMR                                           0.008
#>   RMSEA                                          0.018
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   x1                                             0.860
#>   x2                                             0.683
#>   x3                                             0.772
#>   z1                                             0.839
#>   z2                                             0.694
#>   z3                                             0.759
#>   y1                                             0.838
#>   y2                                             0.726
#>   y3                                             0.750
#>   w1                                             0.840
#>   w2                                             0.695
#>   w3                                             0.771
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   Y                                              0.533
#>   W                                              0.547
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X =~          
#>     x1              0.927      0.011   84.950    0.000
#>     x2              0.826      0.011   77.099    0.000
#>     x3              0.879      0.007  120.113    0.000
#>   Z =~          
#>     z1              0.916      0.009  107.341    0.000
#>     z2              0.833      0.011   76.056    0.000
#>     z3              0.871      0.010   90.614    0.000
#>   Y =~          
#>     y1              0.915      0.009  101.763    0.000
#>     y2              0.852      0.011   78.990    0.000
#>     y3              0.866      0.013   65.884    0.000
#>   W =~          
#>     w1              0.916      0.011   85.177    0.000
#>     w2              0.834      0.014   60.418    0.000
#>     w3              0.878      0.014   64.353    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   Y ~           
#>     X               0.293      0.021   14.044    0.000
#>     Z               0.444      0.035   12.705    0.000
#>   W ~           
#>     X               0.393      0.035   11.208    0.000
#>     Z               0.248      0.047    5.243    0.000
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .Y               0.009      0.005    1.900    0.057
#>    .W               0.009      0.007    1.352    0.176
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X ~~          
#>     Z               0.175      0.012   15.189    0.000
#>   Y~X ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.003      0.005   -0.690    0.490
#>   Y~Z ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.024      0.013   -1.808    0.071
#>     Y~X             0.012      0.007    1.665    0.096
#>   W~X ~~        
#>     W~1             0.003      0.011    0.280    0.779
#>   W~Z ~~        
#>     W~1             0.009      0.009    0.948    0.343
#>     W~X             0.013      0.011    1.172    0.241
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>     X               1.000                             
#>     Z               1.000                             
#>    .Y               0.467      0.026   18.016    0.000
#>    .W               0.453      0.031   14.792    0.000
#>    .x1              0.140      0.020    6.903    0.000
#>    .x2              0.317      0.018   17.923    0.000
#>    .x3              0.228      0.013   17.721    0.000
#>    .z1              0.161      0.016   10.266    0.000
#>    .z2              0.306      0.018   16.798    0.000
#>    .z3              0.241      0.017   14.413    0.000
#>    .y1              0.162      0.016    9.881    0.000
#>    .y2              0.274      0.018   14.954    0.000
#>    .y3              0.250      0.023   11.016    0.000
#>    .w1              0.160      0.020    8.190    0.000
#>    .w2              0.305      0.023   13.384    0.000
#>    .w3              0.229      0.024    9.600    0.000
#>     Y~1             0.079      0.019    4.066    0.000
#>     Y~X             0.018      0.004    4.049    0.000
#>     Y~Z             0.105      0.018    5.944    0.000
#>     W~1             0.054      0.013    4.249    0.000
#>     W~X             0.095      0.016    5.951    0.000
#>     W~Z             0.144      0.027    5.339    0.000

Ordered Indicators 

fit_slopes_ord <- pls(
  slopes_model,
  data      = randomSlopesOrdered,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50,
  ordered   = colnames(randomSlopesOrdered) # explicitly specify variables as ordered
)
summary(fit_slopes_ord)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 3 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                OrdPLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          PROBIT
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                          5000
#>   Number of iterations                               3
#>   Number of latent variables                         4
#>   Number of observed variables                      18
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                   263.372
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                50
#>   SRMR                                           0.010
#>   RMSEA                                          0.029
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   x1                                             0.870
#>   x2                                             0.669
#>   x3                                             0.789
#>   z1                                             0.841
#>   z2                                             0.714
#>   z3                                             0.758
#>   y1                                             0.844
#>   y2                                             0.711
#>   y3                                             0.754
#>   w1                                             0.835
#>   w2                                             0.681
#>   w3                                             0.785
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   Y                                              0.531
#>   W                                              0.544
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X =~          
#>     x1              0.933      0.015   61.433    0.000
#>     x2              0.818      0.010   81.514    0.000
#>     x3              0.888      0.012   76.412    0.000
#>   Z =~          
#>     z1              0.917      0.012   73.869    0.000
#>     z2              0.845      0.014   60.987    0.000
#>     z3              0.871      0.010   84.009    0.000
#>   Y =~          
#>     y1              0.918      0.011   80.310    0.000
#>     y2              0.843      0.012   68.417    0.000
#>     y3              0.869      0.018   47.646    0.000
#>   W =~          
#>     w1              0.914      0.012   75.669    0.000
#>     w2              0.825      0.017   47.878    0.000
#>     w3              0.886      0.014   61.233    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   Y ~           
#>     X               0.290      0.021   13.874    0.000
#>     Z               0.451      0.033   13.505    0.000
#>   W ~           
#>     X               0.391      0.035   11.050    0.000
#>     Z               0.245      0.043    5.720    0.000
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .Y               0.011      0.005    2.317    0.020
#>    .W               0.009      0.006    1.408    0.159
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X ~~          
#>     Z               0.168      0.015   11.126    0.000
#>   Y~X ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.006      0.007   -0.920    0.357
#>   Y~Z ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.024      0.013   -1.878    0.060
#>     Y~X             0.012      0.008    1.560    0.119
#>   W~X ~~        
#>     W~1             0.004      0.011    0.321    0.749
#>   W~Z ~~        
#>     W~1             0.009      0.014    0.671    0.502
#>     W~X             0.016      0.012    1.359    0.174
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>     X               1.000                             
#>     Z               1.000                             
#>    .Y               0.469      0.031   15.215    0.000
#>    .W               0.456      0.033   13.638    0.000
#>    .x1              0.130      0.028    4.607    0.000
#>    .x2              0.331      0.016   20.193    0.000
#>    .x3              0.211      0.021   10.275    0.000
#>    .z1              0.159      0.023    6.982    0.000
#>    .z2              0.286      0.023   12.225    0.000
#>    .z3              0.242      0.018   13.453    0.000
#>    .y1              0.156      0.021    7.428    0.000
#>    .y2              0.289      0.021   13.918    0.000
#>    .y3              0.246      0.032    7.788    0.000
#>    .w1              0.165      0.022    7.512    0.000
#>    .w2              0.319      0.028   11.390    0.000
#>    .w3              0.215      0.026    8.439    0.000
#>     Y~1             0.076      0.017    4.587    0.000
#>     Y~X             0.018      0.005    3.482    0.000
#>     Y~Z             0.101      0.016    6.434    0.000
#>     W~1             0.052      0.011    4.627    0.000
#>     W~X             0.099      0.015    6.797    0.000
#>     W~Z             0.142      0.026    5.478    0.000

Random Intercepts Model 

intercepts_model <- '
  f =~ y1 + y2 + y3
  f ~ x1 + x2 + x3 + w1 + w2 + (1 | cluster)
'

Continuous Indicators 

fit_intercepts_cont <- pls(
  intercepts_model,
  data      = randomIntercepts,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50
)
summary(fit_intercepts_cont)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 2 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                   PLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          LINEAR
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                         10000
#>   Number of iterations                               2
#>   Number of latent variables                         1
#>   Number of observed variables                       9
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                    24.372
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                20
#>   SRMR                                           0.003
#>   RMSEA                                          0.005
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   y1                                             0.891
#>   y2                                             0.785
#>   y3                                             0.814
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   f                                              0.705
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f =~          
#>     y1              0.944      0.007  137.015    0.000
#>     y2              0.886      0.011   81.135    0.000
#>     y3              0.902      0.009   95.665    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f ~           
#>     x1              0.238      0.007   31.758    0.000
#>     x2              0.162      0.007   24.874    0.000
#>     x3              0.077      0.006   13.036    0.000
#>     w1              0.128      0.042    3.067    0.002
#>     w2              0.091      0.043    2.118    0.034
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f              -0.012      0.006   -1.990    0.047
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   x1 ~~         
#>     x2              0.104      0.011    9.261    0.000
#>     x3              0.004      0.012    0.357    0.721
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   x2 ~~         
#>     x3              0.097      0.012    7.761    0.000
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   x3 ~~         
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   w1 ~~         
#>     w2             -0.041      0.050   -0.807    0.420
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f               0.295      0.014   20.849    0.000
#>     x1              1.000                             
#>     x2              1.000                             
#>     x3              1.000                             
#>     w1              1.000                             
#>     w2              1.000                             
#>    .y1              0.109      0.013    8.366    0.000
#>    .y2              0.215      0.019   11.008    0.000
#>    .y3              0.186      0.017   11.002    0.000
#>     f~1             0.582      0.019   29.920    0.000

Ordered Indicators 

fit_intercepts_ord <- pls(
  intercepts_model,
  data      = randomInterceptsOrdered,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50,
  ordered   = colnames(randomInterceptsOrdered) # explicitly specify variables as ordered
)
summary(fit_intercepts_ord)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 2 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                OrdPLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          PROBIT
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                         10000
#>   Number of iterations                               2
#>   Number of latent variables                         1
#>   Number of observed variables                       9
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                    25.508
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                20
#>   SRMR                                           0.003
#>   RMSEA                                          0.005
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   y1                                             0.885
#>   y2                                             0.788
#>   y3                                             0.809
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   f                                              0.684
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f =~          
#>     y1              0.941      0.011   84.966    0.000
#>     y2              0.888      0.014   64.732    0.000
#>     y3              0.899      0.011   85.092    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f ~           
#>     x1              0.241      0.011   22.418    0.000
#>     x2              0.158      0.008   20.298    0.000
#>     x3              0.080      0.007   12.275    0.000
#>     w1              0.121      0.034    3.588    0.000
#>     w2              0.081      0.047    1.739    0.082
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f              -0.011      0.007   -1.594    0.111
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   x1 ~~         
#>     x2              0.110      0.012    9.390    0.000
#>     x3              0.012      0.012    0.951    0.341
#>     w1              0.001      0.003    0.228    0.819
#>     w2              0.001      0.004    0.298    0.766
#>   x2 ~~         
#>     x3              0.099      0.012    8.059    0.000
#>     w1             -0.003      0.002   -1.383    0.167
#>     w2             -0.001      0.003   -0.422    0.673
#>   x3 ~~         
#>     w1             -0.003      0.003   -1.038    0.299
#>     w2              0.003      0.003    0.909    0.363
#>   w1 ~~         
#>     w2             -0.025      0.050   -0.493    0.622
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f               0.316      0.015   21.095    0.000
#>     x1              1.000                             
#>     x2              1.000                             
#>     x3              1.000                             
#>     w1              1.000                             
#>     w2              1.000                             
#>    .y1              0.115      0.021    5.529    0.000
#>    .y2              0.212      0.024    8.691    0.000
#>    .y3              0.191      0.019   10.076    0.000
#>     f~1             0.562      0.019   30.308    0.000
